title: Deviled Eggs
tagline: The party appetizer that disappears first. Every. Single. Time.
description: Hard-boiled eggs with a creamy, tangy yolk filling. Classic, simple, and universally loved.
ingredients: 1 Tbsp white vinegar or pickle juice
steps: Place eggs in a single layer in a saucepan. Cover with cold water by 1 inch.
Bring to a rolling boil over high heat. Remove from heat, cover, and let sit 12 minutes.
Transfer eggs to an ice bath for 5 minutes. Peel under cool running water.
Slice eggs in half lengthwise. Gently remove yolks to a bowl.
Mash yolks with a fork. Add mayo, both mustards, vinegar, salt, and pepper. Mix until smooth.
Pipe or spoon filling into egg white halves.
Sprinkle with paprika and chives. Refrigerate until serving.
notes: Easy peel: Older eggs (1-2 weeks old) peel much easier than fresh eggs.
Steam method: Steam eggs 12 minutes instead of boiling for easier peeling.
Pipe it: Use a zip-lock bag with the corner cut off for a cleaner look.
Make ahead: Fill eggs up to 24 hours ahead. Cover tightly with plastic wrap.
Variations: Add crumbled bacon, relish, hot sauce, or everything bagel seasoning.
